If you wish to allow symbol enhancement, then enable the setting 'Enhance At The Rate Workspace Vector Search With Symbols Context In Chat Mode'.","userText":null,"userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":null,"additionalSvgContent":null,"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-26T11:40:59.857323800Z","documentDataList":null,"listOfFiles":null,"workspaceReferencesFileList":null,"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"@Workspace Getting below error while trying to run the application Exception encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.support.BeanDefinitionOverrideException: Invalid bean definition with name 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]: Cannot register bean definition [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.jdbc.autoconfigure.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] for bean 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' since there is already [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.metadata.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jdbc/metadata/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] bound. Please suggest the fix","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"bc8250e7-5540-4250-9956-7a456859648f","promptLibrary":null,"response":"# Root Cause Analysis and Fix ## Problem Summary The error indicates a **bean definition conflict** where Spring Boot is attempting to register the same bean (`hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider`) twice from two different auto-configuration classes: 1. `org.springframework.boot.jdbc.autoconfigure.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration` (newer package) 2. `org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.metadata.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration` (older package) This conflict arises because: - You have a custom `DataSource` bean in [DatabaseConfig.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java) - Spring Boot's auto-configuration is trying to create metadata providers for HikariCP - There's a classpath conflict with duplicate/incompatible Spring Boot dependencies ## Root Cause Examining [pom.xml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml), I can see: - **Spring Boot version**: 3.4.5 (parent) - **Flyway starter version**: 4.0.6 (explicitly set, line 79) - **Spring Security starter version**: 3.4.12 (explicitly set, line 48) The issue is caused by **version mismatches** where explicitly versioned dependencies are pulling in conflicting transitive dependencies. ## Solution ### Fix 1: Remove Explicit Versions from Dependencies Update [pom.xml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml): ```xml pom.xml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml org.springframework.boot spring-boot-starter-security org.springframework.boot spring-boot-starter-flyway ``` ### Fix 2: Remove Custom DataSource Bean (Recommended) Since Spring Boot 3.x auto-configures HikariCP perfectly with properties, remove the custom bean from [DatabaseConfig.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java): ```java DatabaseConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration; /** * Database configuration for Order Service. * Migrates from legacy H2 in-memory to production PostgreSQL. * * Note: HikariCP is auto-configured by Spring Boot. * Configure via application.properties/application.yml */ @Configuration public class DatabaseConfig { // Remove the custom dataSource() bean // Spring Boot auto-configuration handles HikariCP setup } ``` ### Fix 3: Configure HikariCP via Application Properties Create or update `application.yml` or `application.properties`: ```yaml application.yml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application.yml spring: datasource: url: ${DB_URL:jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/orderdb} username: ${DB_USERNAME:postgres} password: ${DB_PASSWORD:password} driver-class-name: org.postgresql.Driver hikari: maximum-pool-size: 10 minimum-idle: 5 connection-timeout: 30000 idle-timeout: 600000 max-lifetime: 1800000 pool-name: OrderServiceHikariPool ``` ### Alternative Fix: If You Must Keep Custom Bean If you need the custom `DataSource` bean for specific reasons, exclude the auto-configuration: ```java DatabaseConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariConfig; import com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource; import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value; import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.DataSourceAutoConfiguration; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Primary; import javax.sql.DataSource; /** * Database configuration for Order Service. * Migrates from legacy H2 in-memory to production PostgreSQL. */ @Configuration public class DatabaseConfig { @Value("${spring.datasource.url}") private String jdbcUrl; @Value("${spring.datasource.username}") private String username; @Value("${spring.datasource.password}") private String password; @Bean @Primary // Mark as primary to resolve conflicts public DataSource dataSource() { HikariConfig config = new HikariConfig(); config.setJdbcUrl(jdbcUrl); config.setUsername(username); config.setPassword(password); config.setMaximumPoolSize(10); config.setMinimumIdle(5); config.setConnectionTimeout(30000); config.setIdleTimeout(600000); config.setMaxLifetime(1800000); return new HikariDataSource(config); } } ``` And update [OrderServiceApplication.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java): ```java OrderServiceApplication.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java package com.ecommerce.order; import org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication; import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.SpringBootApplication; import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.DataSourceAutoConfiguration; import org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.config.EnableJpaAuditing; @SpringBootApplication(exclude = {DataSourceAutoConfiguration.class}) @EnableJpaAuditing public class OrderServiceApplication { public static void main(String[] args) { SpringApplication.run(OrderServiceApplication.class, args); } } ``` ## Recommended Action Plan 1. **Remove explicit versions** from `spring-boot-starter-security` and `spring-boot-starter-flyway` in [pom.xml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml) 2. **Delete the custom `dataSource()` bean** from [DatabaseConfig.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java) 3. **Configure HikariCP via properties** file 4. Run `mvn clean install` to rebuild 5. Start the application ## Explanation **Why this happens:** - Spring Boot 3.4.5 manages dependency versions through its BOM (Bill of Materials) - Explicitly versioning starters (3.4.12, 4.0.6) creates version conflicts in transitive dependencies - Two different versions of auto-configuration classes attempt to register the same bean - Spring Boot disallows bean overriding by default since 2.1.x **Why the fix works:** - Removing explicit versions ensures all Spring Boot components use compatible versions - Using Spring Boot's auto-configuration eliminates bean definition conflicts - Configuration via properties is the Spring Boot recommended approach and avoids manual bean creation **Trade-offs:** - Relying on auto-configuration means less explicit control but better compatibility - Properties-based configuration is more maintainable and environment-friendly - If custom logic is needed in DataSource creation, the alternative fix with `@Primary` and exclusions works but adds complexity Sources-Repos/Files: - Selected context","userText":"@Workspace Getting below error while trying to run the application Exception encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.support.BeanDefinitionOverrideException: Invalid bean definition with name 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]: Cannot register bean definition [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.jdbc.autoconfigure.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] for bean 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' since there is already [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.metadata.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jdbc/metadata/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] bound. Please suggest the fix","userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":[],"additionalSvgContent":[],"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-26T11:41:06.888737900Z","documentDataList":[],"listOfFiles":["config/DatabaseConfig.java Lines(1-41)","order/config/DatabaseConfig.java"],"workspaceReferencesFileList":["order/service/OrderServiceTest.java Lines(1-33)","order/service/OrderService.java Lines(1-34)","order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java Lines(24-56)","order/config/KafkaProducerConfig.java Lines(1-40)","model/dto/OrderDTO.java Lines(1-41)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(77-103)","order/config/SwaggerConfig.java Lines(1-35)","ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java Lines(1-13)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(1-55)","order/repository/OrderRepository.java Lines(1-39)","order/constants/OrderStatus.java Lines(1-24)","order/repository/OrderItemRepository.java Lines(1-22)","model/entity/OrderItem.java Lines(1-43)","order/service/KafkaEventPublisher.java Lines(1-32)","opus/order-service/pom.xml Lines(1-153)","order/config/DatabaseConfig.java Lines(1-40)","order/config/SecurityConfig.java Lines(1-43)","order/exception/OrderServiceException.java Lines(1-29)","order/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java Lines(1-26)","model/entity/Order.java Lines(1-37)","order/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java Lines(1-80)","order/constants/ApiConstants.java Lines(1-23)","opus/order-service/README.md Lines(1-45)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(131-160)","order/repository/OrderRepositoryTest.java Lines(1-67)","model/dto/OrderItemDTO.java Lines(1-37)"],"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"","selectedFiles":null,"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"a0249e90-7f19-4cb3-a459-2d0eebd2ca08","promptLibrary":null,"response":"Searching Workspace The current workspace search is working without symbol context enhancement. If you wish to allow symbol enhancement, then enable the setting 'Enhance At The Rate Workspace Vector Search With Symbols Context In Chat Mode'.","userText":null,"userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":null,"additionalSvgContent":null,"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-26T11:43:04.227689800Z","documentDataList":null,"listOfFiles":null,"workspaceReferencesFileList":null,"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"@Workspace then Configure HikariCP via properties","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"f0e269cf-7253-4cd3-9aad-45e9e38e122e","promptLibrary":null,"response":"# Configure HikariCP via Application Properties Based on the previous fix recommendation, here's how to configure HikariCP using properties instead of Java configuration: ## Steps Overview 1. **Delete the custom `dataSource()` bean** from DatabaseConfig.java 2. **Create application.yml** with HikariCP configuration 3. **Update pom.xml** to remove explicit dependency versions --- ## 1. Update DatabaseConfig.java Remove the custom bean and keep only the configuration class: ```java DatabaseConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/DatabaseConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration; /** * Database configuration for Order Service. * Migrates from legacy H2 in-memory to production PostgreSQL. * * Note: HikariCP is auto-configured by Spring Boot via application properties. * No manual DataSource bean required - Spring Boot handles it automatically. */ @Configuration public class DatabaseConfig { // Spring Boot auto-configures HikariCP DataSource // Configuration is externalized to application.yml } ``` --- ## 2. Create application.yml Create this file at `src/main/resources/application.yml`: ```yaml application.yml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application.yml spring: application: name: order-service # Database Configuration datasource: url: ${DB_URL:jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/orderdb} username: ${DB_USERNAME:postgres} password: ${DB_PASSWORD:password} driver-class-name: org.postgresql.Driver # HikariCP Configuration hikari: pool-name: OrderServiceHikariPool maximum-pool-size: 10 minimum-idle: 5 connection-timeout: 30000 # 30 seconds idle-timeout: 600000 # 10 minutes max-lifetime: 1800000 # 30 minutes auto-commit: true connection-test-query: SELECT 1 leak-detection-threshold: 60000 # 60 seconds # JPA Configuration jpa: database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect hibernate: ddl-auto: validate # Use Flyway for schema management show-sql: false properties: hibernate: format_sql: true jdbc: batch_size: 20 order_inserts: true order_updates: true # Flyway Configuration flyway: enabled: true baseline-on-migrate: true locations: classpath:db/migration validate-on-migrate: true # Kafka Configuration kafka: bootstrap-servers: ${KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS:localhost:9092} producer: key-serializer: org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer value-serializer: org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.JsonSerializer acks: all retries: 3 # Security Configuration security: oauth2: resourceserver: jwt: issuer-uri: ${JWT_ISSUER_URI:http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/ecommerce} # Server Configuration server: port: ${SERVER_PORT:8081} servlet: context-path: / error: include-message: always include-binding-errors: always # Management/Actuator Configuration management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info,metrics,prometheus endpoint: health: show-details: when-authorized metrics: export: prometheus: enabled: true # Logging Configuration logging: level: com.ecommerce.order: INFO org.springframework.web: INFO org.hibernate.SQL: DEBUG org.hibernate.type.descriptor.sql.BasicBinder: TRACE pattern: console: "%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} - %msg%n" file: "%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n" ``` --- ## 3. Create application-dev.yml (Optional) For development-specific overrides: ```yaml application-dev.yml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application-dev.yml spring: datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/orderdb_dev username: dev_user password: dev_password hikari: maximum-pool-size: 5 minimum-idle: 2 jpa: show-sql: true hibernate: ddl-auto: update # More lenient for dev logging: level: com.ecommerce.order: DEBUG org.springframework.security: DEBUG ``` --- ## 4. Create application-prod.yml (Optional) For production-specific configuration: ```yaml application-prod.yml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application-prod.yml spring: datasource: hikari: maximum-pool-size: 20 minimum-idle: 10 leak-detection-threshold: 30000 jpa: show-sql: false logging: level: com.ecommerce.order: WARN org.springframework.web: WARN ``` --- ## 5. Update pom.xml Remove explicit versions from dependencies (lines 45-48 and 76-79): ```xml pom.xml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml org.springframework.boot spring-boot-starter-security org.springframework.boot spring-boot-starter-flyway ``` --- ## 6. Environment Variables Setup Create `.env` file for local development (add to `.gitignore`): ```properties .env Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/.env # Database Configuration DB_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/orderdb DB_USERNAME=postgres DB_PASSWORD=your_password # Kafka Configuration KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS=localhost:9092 # JWT Configuration JWT_ISSUER_URI=http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/ecommerce # Server Configuration SERVER_PORT=8081 ``` --- ## 7. Run the Application ```bash # Clean and rebuild mvn clean install # Run with default profile mvn spring-boot:run # Run with dev profile mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=dev # Run with prod profile mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=prod ``` --- ## Explanation ### Why This Approach Works 1. **Spring Boot Auto-Configuration**: Spring Boot 3.x automatically detects HikariCP on the classpath and configures it based on `spring.datasource.hikari.*` properties 2. **No Bean Conflicts**: Eliminates the duplicate bean registration error by relying on Spring Boot's auto-configuration 3. **Externalized Configuration**: Properties can be overridden via environment variables, making it cloud-ready 4. **Profile Support**: Different configurations for dev, test, and prod environments 5. **Version Compatibility**: Removing explicit versions ensures all Spring Boot components use compatible versions from the parent BOM ### Key Configuration Properties - **`maximum-pool-size`**: Maximum number of connections in the pool (10 for base, 20 for prod) - **`minimum-idle`**: Minimum idle connections maintained (5 for base, 10 for prod) - **`connection-timeout`**: Max time to wait for a connection (30 seconds) - **`idle-timeout`**: Max time a connection can sit idle (10 minutes) - **`max-lifetime`**: Max lifetime of a connection (30 minutes) - **`leak-detection-threshold`**: Logs warning if connection held longer than this (60 seconds) ### Trade-offs **Advantages:** - ✅ No custom code required - ✅ Environment-specific configuration via profiles - ✅ Easy to override with environment variables - ✅ Follows Spring Boot best practices - ✅ Eliminates bean definition conflicts **Considerations:** - ⚠️ Less explicit control compared to Java configuration - ⚠️ Requires understanding of Spring Boot property hierarchy - ⚠️ Properties scattered across multiple files (can use `@ConfigurationProperties` for grouping if needed) This configuration is production-ready and follows Spring Boot 3.x conventions for microservices deployment. Sources-Repos/Files: - Selected context","userText":"@Workspace then Configure HikariCP via properties","userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":[],"additionalSvgContent":[],"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-26T11:43:07.344564400Z","documentDataList":[],"listOfFiles":["config/DatabaseConfig.java Lines(1-41)","order/config/DatabaseConfig.java"],"workspaceReferencesFileList":["order/service/OrderService.java Lines(1-34)","order/repository/OrderRepository.java Lines(1-39)","order/repository/OrderItemRepository.java Lines(1-22)","order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java Lines(1-56)","model/entity/OrderItem.java Lines(1-43)","order/service/OrderServiceTest.java Lines(1-118)","order/service/KafkaEventPublisher.java Lines(1-32)","order/config/KafkaProducerConfig.java Lines(1-40)","opus/order-service/pom.xml Lines(1-153)","order/config/DatabaseConfig.java Lines(1-40)","order/config/SecurityConfig.java Lines(1-43)","model/dto/OrderDTO.java Lines(1-41)","order/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java Lines(1-26)","order/constants/ApiConstants.java Lines(1-23)","opus/order-service/README.md Lines(1-45)","order/config/SwaggerConfig.java Lines(1-35)","ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java Lines(1-13)","model/entity/Order.java Lines(1-77)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(1-74)","order/repository/OrderRepositoryTest.java Lines(1-76)","model/dto/OrderItemDTO.java Lines(1-37)"],"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"","selectedFiles":null,"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"9678744d-5860-42c8-94df-cd3d6d07a886","promptLibrary":null,"response":"Searching Workspace The current workspace search is working without symbol context enhancement. If you wish to allow symbol enhancement, then enable the setting 'Enhance At The Rate Workspace Vector Search With Symbols Context In Chat Mode'.","userText":null,"userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":null,"additionalSvgContent":null,"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-27T08:08:55.737556Z","documentDataList":null,"listOfFiles":null,"workspaceReferencesFileList":null,"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"@Workspace updateOrderStatus method in OrderController is giving 500 internal server error, while running API","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"15091e1c-ccbd-41e6-a40b-fd459a6cf790","promptLibrary":null,"response":"# Debugging the 500 Internal Server Error in updateOrderStatus ## Quick Diagnosis Based on the code analysis, the 500 error in the `updateOrderStatus` method is likely caused by one of these issues: 1. **Missing `@MockBean` annotation** in the test class causing the service to be null at runtime 2. **Transaction management issue** where the order entity becomes detached 3. **Missing error handling** for the `OrderStatus` enum parsing from the request --- ## Root Cause Analysis ### Issue 1: Service Dependency Injection Problem In [OrderControllerTest.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java) (line 40), the `OrderService` is annotated with `@InjectMocks` instead of `@MockBean`: ```java @InjectMocks private OrderService orderService; ``` This is incorrect for `@WebMvcTest`. The controller expects a real bean, but this creates a mock that isn't registered in the Spring context. ### Issue 2: Potential Transaction Issue In [OrderServiceImpl.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java) (lines 103-113), the `updateOrderStatus` method retrieves an order and updates it: ```java @Override public OrderDTO updateOrderStatus(Long orderId, OrderStatus status) { logger.info("Updating order {} status to {}", orderId, status); Order order = orderRepository.findById(orderId) .orElseThrow(() -> new OrderNotFoundException( String.format(ApiConstants.ORDER_NOT_FOUND, orderId))); order.setStatus(status); Order updatedOrder = orderRepository.save(order); // ... } ``` The method is marked `@Transactional` at the class level (line 25), but if the transaction context is lost or the entity becomes detached, the save operation might fail. ### Issue 3: Missing Validation The [UpdateOrderStatusRequest.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/model/dto/UpdateOrderStatusRequest.java) has validation, but there's no explicit handling for invalid enum values in the controller. --- ## Solution ### Fix 1: Correct the Test Configuration Update [OrderControllerTest.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java): ```java OrderControllerTest.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java package com.ecommerce.order.controller; import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.dto.*; import com.ecommerce.order.service.OrderService; import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired; import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.web.servlet.WebMvcTest; import org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean; // CHANGED: Import MockBean import org.springframework.http.MediaType; import org.springframework.security.test.context.support.WithMockUser; import org.springframework.test.web.servlet.MockMvc; import java.math.BigDecimal; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.util.Arrays; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq; import static org.mockito.Mockito.when; import static org.springframework.security.test.web.servlet.request.SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.csrf; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.*; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.*; /** * Unit tests for OrderController. */ @WebMvcTest(OrderController.class) class OrderControllerTest { @Autowired private MockMvc mockMvc; @Autowired private ObjectMapper objectMapper; @MockBean // CHANGED: Use @MockBean instead of @InjectMocks private OrderService orderService; private CreateOrderRequest createOrderRequest; private CreateOrderResponse createOrderResponse; private OrderDTO orderDTO; @BeforeEach void setUp() { // Setup test data OrderItemDTO item = new OrderItemDTO("ITEM123", 2, new BigDecimal("29.99")); createOrderRequest = new CreateOrderRequest(); createOrderRequest.setCustomerId("CUST123"); createOrderRequest.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); createOrderResponse = new CreateOrderResponse( 1L, "CUST123", OrderStatus.PENDING, new BigDecimal("59.98"), LocalDateTime.now() ); orderDTO = new OrderDTO(); orderDTO.setOrderId(1L); orderDTO.setCustomerId("CUST123"); orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); orderDTO.setTotalAmount(new BigDecimal("59.98")); orderDTO.setOrderDate(LocalDateTime.now()); orderDTO.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testCreateOrder_Success() throws Exception { when(orderService.createOrder(any(CreateOrderRequest.class))) .thenReturn(createOrderResponse); mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(createOrderRequest))) .andExpect(status().isCreated()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("PENDING")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testGetOrderById_Success() throws Exception { when(orderService.getOrderById(1L)).thenReturn(orderDTO); mockMvc.perform(get("/api/orders/1")) .andExpect(status().isOk()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "ADMIN") void testUpdateOrderStatus_Success() throws Exception { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); when(orderService.updateOrderStatus(eq(1L), eq(OrderStatus.SHIPPED))) .thenReturn(orderDTO); UpdateOrderStatusRequest request = new UpdateOrderStatusRequest(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); mockMvc.perform(put("/api/orders/1/status") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(request))) .andExpect(status().isOk()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("SHIPPED")); } @Test void testCreateOrder_Unauthorized() throws Exception { mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(createOrderRequest))) .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized()); } } ``` ### Fix 2: Add Explicit Transaction Handling Update [OrderServiceImpl.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java) to ensure proper transaction management: ```java OrderServiceImpl.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java @Override @Transactional // ADDED: Explicit transaction annotation public OrderDTO updateOrderStatus(Long orderId, OrderStatus status) { logger.info("Updating order {} status to {}", orderId, status); Order order = orderRepository.findById(orderId) .orElseThrow(() -> new OrderNotFoundException( String.format(ApiConstants.ORDER_NOT_FOUND, orderId))); // Validate status transition (optional business logic) validateStatusTransition(order.getStatus(), status); order.setStatus(status); Order updatedOrder = orderRepository.save(order); logger.info("Order {} status updated to {}", orderId, status); return mapToDTO(updatedOrder); } /** * Validate order status transition. * * @param currentStatus Current order status * @param newStatus New order status */ private void validateStatusTransition(OrderStatus currentStatus, OrderStatus newStatus) { // Add business rules for valid status transitions if (currentStatus == OrderStatus.CANCELLED || currentStatus == OrderStatus.RETURNED) { throw new InvalidOrderException( String.format("Cannot update order status from %s to %s", currentStatus, newStatus)); } } ``` ### Fix 3: Add Better Error Handling in Controller Update [OrderController.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderController.java) to add explicit error handling: ```java OrderController.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderController.java @PutMapping("/{id}/status") @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')") @Operation(summary = "Update order status", description = "Update the status of an existing order") @ApiResponses(value = { @ApiResponse(responseCode = "200", description = "Order status updated"), @ApiResponse(responseCode = "404", description = "Order not found"), @ApiResponse(responseCode = "400", description = "Invalid status or transition"), @ApiResponse(responseCode = "401", description = "Unauthorized"), @ApiResponse(responseCode = "403", description = "Forbidden") }) public ResponseEntity updateOrderStatus( @PathVariable Long id, @Valid @RequestBody UpdateOrderStatusRequest request) { logger.info("Updating order status for ID: {} to {}", id, request.getStatus()); try { OrderDTO updatedOrder = orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, request.getStatus()); return ResponseEntity.ok(updatedOrder); } catch (OrderNotFoundException e) { logger.error("Order not found: {}", id, e); throw e; // Let GlobalExceptionHandler handle it } catch (InvalidOrderException e) { logger.error("Invalid order status update: {}", e.getMessage(), e); throw e; // Let GlobalExceptionHandler handle it } catch (Exception e) { logger.error("Unexpected error updating order status for ID: {}", id, e); throw new OrderServiceException("Failed to update order status", e); } } ``` ### Fix 4: Enhance Global Exception Handler Update [GlobalExceptionHandler.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java) to log more details: ```java GlobalExceptionHandler.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) public ResponseEntity handleGenericException(Exception ex) { logger.error("Unexpected error occurred", ex); // ADDED: Full stack trace logging ErrorResponse error = new ErrorResponse( HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.value(), "An unexpected error occurred: " + ex.getMessage(), LocalDateTime.now() ); return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR).body(error); } ``` --- ## Debugging Steps ### Step 1: Check Application Logs Look for the actual exception in your application logs. The 500 error should have a stack trace showing: - The exact exception type - The line number where it failed - The root cause ### Step 2: Test with Curl/Postman ```bash curl -X PUT http://localhost:8081/api/orders/1/status \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -d '{"status": "SHIPPED"}' ``` ### Step 3: Verify Database Connection Ensure the order with the given ID exists: ```sql SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_id = 1; ``` ### Step 4: Check Security Configuration Verify that the JWT token has the `ADMIN` role required by the `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")` annotation. --- ## Explanation ### Why These Fixes Work 1. **@MockBean vs @InjectMocks**: `@WebMvcTest` requires beans to be registered in the Spring context. `@MockBean` creates a mock and registers it, while `@InjectMocks` only creates a mock instance without Spring context registration. 2. **Explicit @Transactional**: While the class-level annotation should work, adding it explicitly to the method ensures the transaction boundary is clear and prevents entity detachment issues. 3. **Enhanced Error Handling**: Wrapping the service call in try-catch allows you to log the actual exception before it's handled by the global exception handler, making debugging easier. 4. **Status Validation**: Adding business logic validation prevents invalid state transitions that could cause database constraint violations. ### Common Causes of 500 Errors in This Scenario - **NullPointerException**: Service not injected properly - **LazyInitializationException**: Accessing lazy-loaded collections outside transaction - **ConstraintViolationException**: Database constraint violations - **IllegalArgumentException**: Invalid enum value parsing - **Authentication/Authorization failures**: Missing or invalid JWT token ### Next Steps If the error persists after these fixes: 1. **Enable debug logging** in `application.yml`: ```yaml logging: level: com.ecommerce.order: DEBUG org.springframework.web: DEBUG org.hibernate: DEBUG ``` 2. **Check the actual error response** from the API to see if `GlobalExceptionHandler` is catching it 3. **Verify the request payload** matches the expected format exactly 4. **Test the service layer directly** with a unit test to isolate whether the issue is in the controller or service layer Sources-Repos/Files: - Selected context","userText":"@Workspace updateOrderStatus method in OrderController is giving 500 internal server error, while running API","userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":[],"additionalSvgContent":[],"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-05-27T08:09:04.849488700Z","documentDataList":[],"listOfFiles":["order/controller/OrderController.java","controller/OrderController.java Lines(1-147)","controller/OrderController.java Lines(113-113)"],"workspaceReferencesFileList":["order/service/OrderServiceTest.java Lines(1-33)","order/constants/OrderStatus.java Lines(1-24)","order/service/OrderServiceTest.java Lines(64-124)","model/dto/OrderDTO.java Lines(1-41)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(77-103)","order/controller/OrderController.java Lines(144-147)","order/exception/OrderServiceException.java Lines(1-29)","model/entity/Order.java Lines(1-124)","model/dto/UpdateOrderStatusRequest.java Lines(1-29)","opus/order-service/README.md Lines(1-33)","order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java Lines(1-125)","order/repository/OrderRepository.java Lines(1-42)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(1-74)","order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java Lines(105-160)","order/service/OrderService.java Lines(1-49)","order/controller/OrderController.java Lines(70-142)","model/dto/CreateOrderResponse.java Lines(1-74)","order/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java Lines(1-53)","order/repository/OrderRepositoryTest.java Lines(1-67)","order/controller/OrderController.java Lines(1-68)"],"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null,"selectedLocalPromptNames":null},{"prompt":"I think step 4 is causing the issue. How to check JWT token has ADMIN access?","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"7603e564-e5d1-45dc-85f2-90753763ce23","promptLibrary":null,"response":"# Checking JWT Token for ADMIN Access Based on the error context from your previous conversation about the `updateOrderStatus` method returning a 500 error, **Step 4** refers to verifying that the JWT token has the required `ADMIN` role. Here's how to check and fix this: ## Quick Answer 1. **The JWT token must contain the `ADMIN` role** in its claims 2. **Spring Security's `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")` validates this automatically** 3. **The `JwtAuthenticationFilter` must extract roles from the token and set them in the SecurityContext** --- ## Current Issue Analysis Looking at [OrderController.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderController.java) line 105-113, the `updateOrderStatus` endpoint requires ADMIN role: ```java @PutMapping("/{id}/status") @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')") // This checks for ADMIN role public ResponseEntity updateOrderStatus( @PathVariable Long id, @Valid @RequestBody UpdateOrderStatusRequest request) { // ... } ``` The problem is likely in [JwtAuthenticationFilter.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java) - it's incomplete and doesn't properly extract roles from the JWT token. --- ## Solution: Complete JWT Authentication Filter Update [JwtAuthenticationFilter.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java) to properly extract and validate roles: ```java JwtAuthenticationFilter.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java package com.ecommerce.order.security; import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper; import jakarta.servlet.FilterChain; import jakarta.servlet.ServletException; import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest; import jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse; import org.slf4j.Logger; import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory; import org.springframework.security.authentication.UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken; import org.springframework.security.core.authority.SimpleGrantedAuthority; import org.springframework.security.core.context.SecurityContextHolder; import org.springframework.stereotype.Component; import org.springframework.util.StringUtils; import org.springframework.web.filter.OncePerRequestFilter; import java.io.IOException; import java.util.Base64; import java.util.List; import java.util.Map; import java.util.stream.Collectors; /** * JWT authentication filter. * Maps from legacy container-managed authentication to JWT-based auth. */ @Component public class JwtAuthenticationFilter extends OncePerRequestFilter { private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JwtAuthenticationFilter.class); private static final String AUTHORIZATION_HEADER = "Authorization"; private static final String BEARER_PREFIX = "Bearer "; private final ObjectMapper objectMapper = new ObjectMapper(); @Override protected void doFilterInternal(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response, FilterChain filterChain) throws ServletException, IOException { try { String jwt = extractJwtFromRequest(request); if (StringUtils.hasText(jwt)) { // Parse JWT and extract claims Map claims = parseJwtClaims(jwt); // Extract username String username = (String) claims.get("sub"); // Extract roles from JWT List authorities = extractAuthorities(claims); // Log for debugging logger.debug("Authenticated user: {}, roles: {}", username, authorities); // Create authentication token UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken authentication = new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(username, null, authorities); // Set authentication in SecurityContext SecurityContextHolder.getContext().setAuthentication(authentication); } } catch (Exception e) { logger.error("Cannot set user authentication: {}", e.getMessage()); // Don't throw exception - let the request proceed without authentication // Spring Security will handle unauthorized access } filterChain.doFilter(request, response); } /** * Extract JWT token from Authorization header. */ private String extractJwtFromRequest(HttpServletRequest request) { String bearerToken = request.getHeader(AUTHORIZATION_HEADER); if (StringUtils.hasText(bearerToken) && bearerToken.startsWith(BEARER_PREFIX)) { return bearerToken.substring(BEARER_PREFIX.length()); } return null; } /** * Parse JWT claims (simplified - decode payload without signature verification). * * NOTE: In production, use a proper JWT library like jjwt or spring-security-oauth2-resource-server * to validate signatures and expiration. */ private Map parseJwtClaims(String jwt) throws IOException { String[] parts = jwt.split("\\."); if (parts.length < 2) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid JWT token format"); } // Decode payload (second part) String payload = new String(Base64.getUrlDecoder().decode(parts[1])); // Parse JSON return objectMapper.readValue(payload, Map.class); } /** * Extract authorities/roles from JWT claims. * * Supports multiple claim formats: * - "roles": ["ADMIN", "USER"] * - "authorities": ["ROLE_ADMIN", "ROLE_USER"] * - "realm_access": { "roles": ["ADMIN"] } (Keycloak format) */ @SuppressWarnings("unchecked") private List extractAuthorities(Map claims) { // Try "roles" claim first if (claims.containsKey("roles")) { List roles = (List) claims.get("roles"); return roles.stream() .map(role -> new SimpleGrantedAuthority("ROLE_" + role)) .collect(Collectors.toList()); } // Try "authorities" claim if (claims.containsKey("authorities")) { List authorities = (List) claims.get("authorities"); return authorities.stream() .map(SimpleGrantedAuthority::new) .collect(Collectors.toList()); } // Try Keycloak format: realm_access.roles if (claims.containsKey("realm_access")) { Map realmAccess = (Map) claims.get("realm_access"); if (realmAccess.containsKey("roles")) { List roles = (List) realmAccess.get("roles"); return roles.stream() .map(role -> new SimpleGrantedAuthority("ROLE_" + role)) .collect(Collectors.toList()); } } // Default: no authorities logger.warn("No roles/authorities found in JWT claims"); return List.of(); } } ``` --- ## How to Test JWT Token for ADMIN Role ### Method 1: Decode JWT Token Manually Use [jwt.io](https://jwt.io) to decode your token and verify the payload contains: ```json { "sub": "user123", "roles": ["ADMIN"], "exp": 1234567890 } ``` Or in Keycloak format: ```json { "sub": "user123", "realm_access": { "roles": ["ADMIN", "USER"] }, "exp": 1234567890 } ``` ### Method 2: Add Debug Logging Add this to [application.yml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application.yml): ```yaml logging: level: com.ecommerce.order.security: DEBUG org.springframework.security: DEBUG ``` Then check logs for: ``` Authenticated user: user123, roles: [ROLE_ADMIN] ``` ### Method 3: Test with Curl ```bash # Extract your JWT token TOKEN="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..." # Test the endpoint curl -X PUT http://localhost:8081/api/orders/1/status \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \ -d '{"status": "SHIPPED"}' \ -v ``` **Expected responses:** - ✅ **200 OK**: Token has ADMIN role - ❌ **403 Forbidden**: Token exists but lacks ADMIN role - ❌ **401 Unauthorized**: Token is missing or invalid --- ## Production-Ready Alternative: Use Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server For production, replace the custom filter with Spring Security's built-in JWT support: ### Update [pom.xml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml): ```xml org.springframework.boot spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server ``` ### Update [SecurityConfig.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/SecurityConfig.java): ```java SecurityConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/SecurityConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration; import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.method.configuration.EnableMethodSecurity; import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity; import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.configuration.EnableWebSecurity; import org.springframework.security.config.http.SessionCreationPolicy; import org.springframework.security.core.authority.SimpleGrantedAuthority; import org.springframework.security.oauth2.server.resource.authentication.JwtAuthenticationConverter; import org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain; import java.util.Collection; import java.util.List; import java.util.Map; import java.util.stream.Collectors; @Configuration @EnableWebSecurity @EnableMethodSecurity public class SecurityConfig { @Bean public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception { http .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable()) .sessionManagement(session -> session.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS)) .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth .requestMatchers("/actuator/**", "/swagger-ui/**", "/v3/api-docs/**").permitAll() .requestMatchers("/api/orders/**").authenticated() .anyRequest().authenticated() ) .oauth2ResourceServer(oauth2 -> oauth2 .jwt(jwt -> jwt.jwtAuthenticationConverter(jwtAuthenticationConverter())) ); return http.build(); } /** * Convert JWT claims to Spring Security authorities. */ @Bean public JwtAuthenticationConverter jwtAuthenticationConverter() { JwtAuthenticationConverter converter = new JwtAuthenticationConverter(); converter.setJwtGrantedAuthoritiesConverter(jwt -> { // Extract roles from JWT claims Map realmAccess = jwt.getClaim("realm_access"); if (realmAccess != null && realmAccess.containsKey("roles")) { @SuppressWarnings("unchecked") List roles = (List) realmAccess.get("roles"); return roles.stream() .map(role -> new SimpleGrantedAuthority("ROLE_" + role)) .collect(Collectors.toList()); } // Fallback: try "roles" claim directly Collection roles = jwt.getClaim("roles"); if (roles != null) { return roles.stream() .map(role -> new SimpleGrantedAuthority("ROLE_" + role)) .collect(Collectors.toList()); } return List.of(); }); return converter; } } ``` ### Update [application.yml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/resources/application.yml): ```yaml spring: security: oauth2: resourceserver: jwt: issuer-uri: ${JWT_ISSUER_URI:http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/ecommerce} # Or use jwk-set-uri for direct JWK endpoint # jwk-set-uri: ${JWT_JWK_SET_URI:http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/ecommerce/protocol/openid-connect/certs} ``` --- ## Explanation ### Why Step 4 Fails The `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")` annotation checks if the current `Authentication` object in `SecurityContextHolder` has a `GrantedAuthority` with the name `ROLE_ADMIN`. If: 1. **JWT token doesn't contain roles** → No authorities set → Access denied 2. **Filter doesn't extract roles** → Empty authorities → Access denied 3. **Role name mismatch** (e.g., token has `"admin"` but Spring expects `"ROLE_ADMIN"`) → Access denied ### How the Fix Works 1. **JwtAuthenticationFilter** extracts the JWT from the `Authorization` header 2. **Parses the payload** to get claims (username, roles, etc.) 3. **Converts roles to `GrantedAuthority`** objects with `ROLE_` prefix 4. **Sets authentication in SecurityContext** so Spring Security can access it 5. **`@PreAuthorize` checks** if `ROLE_ADMIN` exists in the authorities ### Security Considerations - ✅ **Signature verification**: Production code should validate JWT signatures using the issuer's public key - ✅ **Expiration check**: Validate `exp` claim to reject expired tokens - ✅ **Issuer validation**: Verify `iss` claim matches expected issuer - ✅ **Use OAuth2 Resource Server**: Spring's built-in support handles all of the above automatically The custom filter provided is simplified for debugging. For production, use the OAuth2 Resource Server approach with proper signature validation. Sources-Repos/Files: - Selected context","userText":"I think step 4 is causing the issue. OrderRepositoryTest.java 1. **Added `testFindById_Success()`** - Tests successful retrieval 2. **Added `testFindById_NotFound()`** - Tests not found scenario 3. **Added `testSaveOrder()`** - Tests save operation 4. **Added `testUpdateOrderStatus()`** - Tests update operation with entity refresh --- ## Running the Tests ```bash # Run all tests mvn test # Run specific test class mvn test -Dtest=OrderControllerTest mvn test -Dtest=OrderServiceTest mvn test -Dtest=OrderRepositoryTest # Run with coverage mvn clean test jacoco:report ``` --- ## Explanation **Why the original tests failed:** 1. **`@InjectMocks` in `@WebMvcTest`** - This annotation creates a mock instance but doesn't register it in Spring's application context. `@WebMvcTest` requires beans to be registered via `@MockBean` so the controller can autowire them. 2. **Kafka verification mismatch** - The test verified `publishOrderCreatedEvent()` was called, but the actual implementation has this line commented out (line 70 in [OrderServiceImpl.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/service/OrderServiceImpl.java)). 3. **Missing test coverage** - Several edge cases and error scenarios weren't tested, which could cause failures in integration testing. **How these fixes work:** - `@MockBean` creates a mock and registers it in the Spring test context, allowing proper dependency injection - Removing the Kafka verification aligns the test with the actual implementation - Additional test cases improve coverage and catch potential bugs early import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.dto.*; import com.ecommerce.order.service.OrderService; import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired; import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.web.servlet.WebMvcTest; import org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockitoBean; // CHANGED: Import MockitoBean import org.springframework.http.MediaType; import org.springframework.security.test.context.support.WithMockUser; import org.springframework.test.web.servlet.MockMvc; import java.math.BigDecimal; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.util.Arrays; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq; import static org.mockito.Mockito.when; import static org.springframework.security.test.web.servlet.request.SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.csrf; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.*; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.*; /** * Unit tests for OrderController. */ @WebMvcTest(OrderController.class) class OrderControllerTest { @Autowired private MockMvc mockMvc; @Autowired private ObjectMapper objectMapper; @MockitoBean // CHANGED: Use @MockitoBean instead of @MockBean private OrderService orderService; private CreateOrderRequest createOrderRequest; private CreateOrderResponse createOrderResponse; private OrderDTO orderDTO; @BeforeEach void setUp() { // Setup test data OrderItemDTO item = new OrderItemDTO("ITEM123", 2, new BigDecimal("29.99")); createOrderRequest = new CreateOrderRequest(); createOrderRequest.setCustomerId("CUST123"); createOrderRequest.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); createOrderResponse = new CreateOrderResponse( 1L, "CUST123", OrderStatus.PENDING, new BigDecimal("59.98"), LocalDateTime.now() ); orderDTO = new OrderDTO(); orderDTO.setOrderId(1L); orderDTO.setCustomerId("CUST123"); orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); orderDTO.setTotalAmount(new BigDecimal("59.98")); orderDTO.setOrderDate(LocalDateTime.now()); orderDTO.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testCreateOrder_Success() throws Exception { when(orderService.createOrder(any(CreateOrderRequest.class))) .thenReturn(createOrderResponse); mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(createOrderRequest))) .andExpect(status().isCreated()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("PENDING")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testGetOrderById_Success() throws Exception { when(orderService.getOrderById(1L)).thenReturn(orderDTO); mockMvc.perform(get("/api/orders/1")) .andExpect(status().isOk()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "ADMIN") void testUpdateOrderStatus_Success() throws Exception { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); when(orderService.updateOrderStatus(eq(1L), eq(OrderStatus.SHIPPED))) .thenReturn(orderDTO); UpdateOrderStatusRequest request = new UpdateOrderStatusRequest(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); mockMvc.perform(put("/api/orders/1/status") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(request))) .andExpect(status().isOk()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("SHIPPED")); } @Test void testCreateOrder_Unauthorized() throws Exception { mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(createOrderRequest))) .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized()); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testProcessReturn_Success() throws Exception { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.RETURNED); when(orderService.processReturn(1L)).thenReturn(orderDTO); mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders/1/return") .with(csrf())) .andExpect(status().isOk()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("RETURNED")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testUpdateOrderStatus_Forbidden() throws Exception { UpdateOrderStatusRequest request = new UpdateOrderStatusRequest(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); mockMvc.perform(put("/api/orders/1/status") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(request))) .andExpect(status().isForbidden()); } } ``` --- ## Alternative Approach: Pure Mockito Tests (Without Spring Context) If you don't need the full Spring MVC test context, use pure Mockito: ```java OrderControllerTest.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java package com.ecommerce.order.controller; import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.dto.*; import com.ecommerce.order.service.OrderService; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; import org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith; import org.mockito.InjectMocks; import org.mockito.Mock; import org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension; import org.springframework.http.HttpStatus; import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity; import java.math.BigDecimal; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.util.Arrays; import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq; import static org.mockito.Mockito.*; /** * Unit tests for OrderController (Pure Mockito - no Spring context). */ @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) class OrderControllerTest { @Mock private OrderService orderService; @InjectMocks private OrderController orderController; private CreateOrderRequest createOrderRequest; private CreateOrderResponse createOrderResponse; private OrderDTO orderDTO; @BeforeEach void setUp() { OrderItemDTO item = new OrderItemDTO("ITEM123", 2, new BigDecimal("29.99")); createOrderRequest = new CreateOrderRequest(); createOrderRequest.setCustomerId("CUST123"); createOrderRequest.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); createOrderResponse = new CreateOrderResponse( 1L, "CUST123", OrderStatus.PENDING, new BigDecimal("59.98"), LocalDateTime.now() ); orderDTO = new OrderDTO(); orderDTO.setOrderId(1L); orderDTO.setCustomerId("CUST123"); orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); orderDTO.setTotalAmount(new BigDecimal("59.98")); orderDTO.setOrderDate(LocalDateTime.now()); orderDTO.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); } @Test void testCreateOrder_Success() { when(orderService.createOrder(any(CreateOrderRequest.class))) .thenReturn(createOrderResponse); ResponseEntity response = orderController.createOrder(createOrderRequest, "correlation-123"); assertNotNull(response); assertEquals(HttpStatus.CREATED, response.getStatusCode()); assertEquals(1L, response.getBody().getOrderId()); assertEquals("CUST123", response.getBody().getCustomerId()); verify(orderService, times(1)).createOrder(any(CreateOrderRequest.class)); } @Test void testGetOrderById_Success() { when(orderService.getOrderById(1L)).thenReturn(orderDTO); ResponseEntity response = orderController.getOrderById(1L); assertNotNull(response); assertEquals(HttpStatus.OK, response.getStatusCode()); assertEquals(1L, response.getBody().getOrderId()); verify(orderService, times(1)).getOrderById(1L); } @Test void testUpdateOrderStatus_Success() { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); when(orderService.updateOrderStatus(eq(1L), eq(OrderStatus.SHIPPED))) .thenReturn(orderDTO); UpdateOrderStatusRequest request = new UpdateOrderStatusRequest(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); ResponseEntity response = orderController.updateOrderStatus(1L, request); assertNotNull(response); assertEquals(HttpStatus.OK, response.getStatusCode()); assertEquals(OrderStatus.SHIPPED, response.getBody().getStatus()); verify(orderService, times(1)).updateOrderStatus(1L, OrderStatus.SHIPPED); } @Test void testProcessReturn_Success() { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.RETURNED); when(orderService.processReturn(1L)).thenReturn(orderDTO); ResponseEntity response = orderController.processReturn(1L); assertNotNull(response); assertEquals(HttpStatus.OK, response.getStatusCode()); assertEquals(OrderStatus.RETURNED, response.getBody().getStatus()); verify(orderService, times(1)).processReturn(1L); } } ``` --- ## Comparison: @MockitoBean vs Pure Mockito | Feature | @MockitoBean (with @WebMvcTest) | Pure Mockito (@Mock) | |---------|--------------------------------|---------------------| | **Spring Context** | ✅ Full Spring MVC context loaded | ❌ No Spring context | | **MockMvc** | ✅ Available for HTTP testing | ❌ Not available | | **Security Testing** | ✅ Can use @WithMockUser | ❌ Manual security setup | | **Test Speed** | ⚠️ Slower (context loading) | ✅ Faster (no context) | | **Use Case** | Integration-style controller tests | Pure unit tests | --- ## Explanation ### Why @MockBean Was Deprecated - **Spring Boot 3.4+** deprecated `@MockBean` in favor of `@MockitoBean` for better naming consistency - The functionality is identical; only the annotation name changed - `@MockitoBean` makes it clearer that it's using Mockito under the hood ### When to Use Each Approach **Use `@MockitoBean` with `@WebMvcTest` when:** - Testing HTTP request/response handling - Validating request mappings, status codes, JSON serialization - Testing security configurations (@PreAuthorize, etc.) - Need MockMvc for integration-style tests **Use pure Mockito (`@Mock` + `@ExtendWith`) when:** - Testing controller logic in isolation - Don't need HTTP layer testing - Want faster test execution - Testing business logic without Spring overhead ### Migration Path 1. **Find and replace** all `@MockBean` with `@MockitoBean` 2. **Update imports** from `org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean` to `org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockitoBean` 3. **No code changes needed** - the behavior is identical The updated test file maintains all existing test coverage while using the non-deprecated annotation. argument","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"c96257cc-ab8b-4ba4-aed4-c0bc5e49d59b","promptLibrary":null,"response":"# Fixing JPA Auditing Bean Creation Error in Tests ## Root Cause Analysis The error `Cannot resolve reference to bean 'jpaMappingContext' while setting constructor argument` occurs because: 1. **`@WebMvcTest` loads only web layer components** - it doesn't load JPA repositories or entity scanning 2. **`@EnableJpaAuditing` in the main application class** requires JPA context to be initialized 3. **Test context doesn't have JPA infrastructure** needed for auditing Looking at [OrderServiceApplication.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java) line 7, `@EnableJpaAuditing` is enabled at the application level, but `@WebMvcTest` doesn't load the full JPA context. --- ## Solution: Exclude JPA Auditing from Test Context ### Option 1: Create Separate JPA Auditing Configuration (Recommended) **Step 1:** Create a new configuration class for JPA Auditing ```java JpaAuditingConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/JpaAuditingConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration; import org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.config.EnableJpaAuditing; /** * JPA Auditing configuration. * Separated from main application class to allow exclusion in web layer tests. */ @Configuration @EnableJpaAuditing public class JpaAuditingConfig { } ``` **Step 2:** Remove `@EnableJpaAuditing` from main application class ```java OrderServiceApplication.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/main/java/com/ecommerce/order/OrderServiceApplication.java package com.ecommerce.order; import org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication; import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.SpringBootApplication; @SpringBootApplication // REMOVED: @EnableJpaAuditing - moved to JpaAuditingConfig public class OrderServiceApplication { public static void main(String[] args) { SpringApplication.run(OrderServiceApplication.class, args); } } ``` **Step 3:** Exclude JPA Auditing configuration in test ```java OrderControllerTest.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java package com.ecommerce.order.controller; import com.ecommerce.order.config.JpaAuditingConfig; import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.dto.*; import com.ecommerce.order.service.OrderService; import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired; import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.web.servlet.WebMvcTest; import org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean; import org.springframework.context.annotation.ComponentScan; import org.springframework.context.annotation.FilterType; import org.springframework.http.MediaType; import org.springframework.security.test.context.support.WithMockUser; import org.springframework.test.web.servlet.MockMvc; import java.math.BigDecimal; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.util.Arrays; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any; import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq; import static org.mockito.Mockito.when; import static org.springframework.security.test.web.servlet.request.SecurityMockMvcRequestPostProcessors.csrf; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.*; import static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.*; /** * Unit tests for OrderController. */ @WebMvcTest( controllers = OrderController.class, excludeFilters = @ComponentScan.Filter( type = FilterType.ASSIGNABLE_TYPE, classes = JpaAuditingConfig.class // ADDED: Exclude JPA Auditing ) ) class OrderControllerTest { @Autowired private MockMvc mockMvc; @Autowired private ObjectMapper objectMapper; @MockBean private OrderService orderService; private CreateOrderRequest createOrderRequest; private CreateOrderResponse createOrderResponse; private OrderDTO orderDTO; @BeforeEach void setUp() { // Setup test data OrderItemDTO item = new OrderItemDTO("ITEM123", 2, new BigDecimal("29.99")); createOrderRequest = new CreateOrderRequest(); createOrderRequest.setCustomerId("CUST123"); createOrderRequest.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); createOrderResponse = new CreateOrderResponse( 1L, "CUST123", OrderStatus.PENDING, new BigDecimal("59.98"), LocalDateTime.now() ); orderDTO = new OrderDTO(); orderDTO.setOrderId(1L); orderDTO.setCustomerId("CUST123"); orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); orderDTO.setTotalAmount(new BigDecimal("59.98")); orderDTO.setOrderDate(LocalDateTime.now()); orderDTO.setItems(Arrays.asList(item)); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void testCreateOrder_Success() throws Exception { when(orderService.createOrder(any(CreateOrderRequest.class))) .thenReturn(createOrderResponse); mockMvc.perform(post("/api/orders") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(createOrderRequest))) .andExpect(status().isCreated()) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.status").value("PENDING")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "CUSTOMER") void .andExpect(jsonPath("$.orderId").value(1L)) .andExpect(jsonPath("$.customerId").value("CUST123")); } @Test @WithMockUser(roles = "ADMIN") void testUpdateOrderStatus_Success() throws Exception { orderDTO.setStatus(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); when(orderService.updateOrderStatus(eq(1L), eq(OrderStatus.SHIPPED))) .thenReturn(orderDTO); UpdateOrderStatusRequest request = new UpdateOrderStatusRequest(OrderStatus.SHIPPED); mockMvc.perform(put("/api/orders/1/status") .with(csrf()) .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) .content(objectMapper.writeValueAsString(request))) datasource class OrderControllerTest { @Autowired private MockMvc mockMvc; @Autowired private ObjectMapper objectMapper; @MockBean private OrderService orderService; // ... rest of the test code remains the same } ``` --- ### Option 3: Disable JPA Auditing in Test Profile Create a test-specific configuration: ```java TestConfig.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/config/TestConfig.java package com.ecommerce.order.config; import org.springframework.boot.test.context.TestConfiguration; import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean; import org.springframework.data.domain.AuditorAware; import org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.config.EnableJpaAuditing; import java.util.Optional; /** * Test configuration to provide minimal JPA auditing setup. */ @TestConfiguration @EnableJpaAuditing public class TestConfig { @Bean public AuditorAware auditorProvider() { return () -> Optional.of("test-user"); } } ``` Then import it in your test: ```java OrderControllerTest.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/controller/OrderControllerTest.java @WebMvcTest(OrderController.class) @Import(TestConfig.class) // ADDED: Import test configuration class OrderControllerTest { // ... test code } ``` --- ## Recommended Approach **Use Option 1** (Separate JPA Auditing Configuration) because: ✅ **Clean separation of concerns** - auditing configuration is isolated ✅ **Easy to exclude in tests** - no need for additional test infrastructure ✅ **Production code unchanged** - only organizational refactoring ✅ **Follows Spring Boot best practices** - configuration classes for specific features --- ## Verification Steps After applying the fix: ```bash # Clean and rebuild mvn clean install # Run only the failing test mvn test -Dtest=OrderControllerTest # Run all tests mvn test # Verify with verbose output mvn test -X ``` --- ## Explanation ### Why This Error Occurs 1. **`@WebMvcTest` is a slice test** - it only loads web layer components (controllers, filters, etc.) 2. **JPA infrastructure not loaded** - no `EntityManager`, repositories, or JPA context 3. **`@EnableJpaAuditing` requires `jpaMappingContext`** - a bean created by JPA auto-configuration 4. **Bean creation fails** - Spring tries to create `jpaAuditingHandler` but can't find its dependency ### How the Fix Works **Option 1 (Recommended):** - Moves `@EnableJpaAuditing` to a separate `@Configuration` class - Uses `excludeFilters` in `@WebMvcTest` to skip loading this configuration - Test runs without JPA context, which is fine since we're mocking the service layer **Option 2:** - Adds `@AutoConfigureDataJpa` to enable minimal JPA infrastructure in the test - Provides enough context for auditing to work without full application context **Option 3:** - Creates a test-specific configuration with minimal auditing setup - Imports it explicitly in tests that need it ### Trade-offs | Approach | Pros | Cons | |----------|------|------| | **Option 1** | ✅ Clean, no test overhead | ⚠️ Requires code reorganization | | **Option 2** | ✅ Quick fix, minimal changes | ⚠️ Adds JPA overhead to web tests | | **Option 3** | ✅ Flexible, test-specific | ⚠️ Requires test configuration maintenance | The recommended **Option 1** provides the cleanest solution with proper separation of concerns and no performance impact on web layer tests. This happens because: 1. **`@DataJpaTest` loads Flyway auto-configuration** by default 2. **Test database (H2) may have schema incompatibilities** with PostgreSQL-specific SQL 3. **Migration scripts are designed for PostgreSQL**, not the in-memory H2 database used in tests --- ## Solution: Disable Flyway in Tests ### Option 1: Disable Flyway for All Tests (Recommended) Create a test-specific configuration file: ```yaml application-test.yml src/test/resources/application-test.yml spring: # Disable Flyway for tests flyway: enabled: false # Use H2 in-memory database for tests datasource: url: jdbc:h2:mem:testdb;MODE=PostgreSQL;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;DB_CLOSE_ON_EXIT=FALSE driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver username: sa password: # Let Hibernate create schema from entities jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: create-drop show-sql: true properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect format_sql: true # Disable Flyway validation flyway: enabled: false ``` ### Option 2: Disable Flyway Per Test Class Update [OrderRepositoryTest.java](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/repository/OrderRepositoryTest.java): ```java OrderRepositoryTest.java Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/repository/OrderRepositoryTest.java package com.ecommerce.order.repository; import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.entity.Order; import com.ecommerce.order.model.entity.OrderItem; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import java.util.List; import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*; /** * Integration tests for OrderRepository. */ @DataJpaTest @TestPropertySource(properties = { "spring.flyway.enabled=false", // ADDED: Disable Flyway "spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=create-drop" // ADDED: Let Hibernate manage schema }) class OrderRepositoryTest { @Autowired private TestEntityManager entityManager; @Autowired assertEquals(1, orders.size()); assertEquals(OrderStatus.PENDING, orders.get(0).getStatus()); } @Test void testFindByCustomerIdAndStatus() { List orders = orderRepository.findByCustomerIdAndStatus( dependency to [pom.xml](Downloads/java%20legacy%20migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml): ```xml pom.xml Downloads/java legacy migration/opus/order-service/pom.xml org.testcontainers postgresql test org.testcontainers junit-jupiter test ``` **Step 2:** Create base test class: ```java AbstractIntegrationTest.java src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/AbstractIntegrationTest.java package com.ecommerce.order; import org.springframework.boot.test.context.SpringBootTest; import org.springframework.test.context.DynamicPropertyRegistry; import org.springframework.test.context.DynamicPropertySource; import org.testcontainers.containers.PostgreSQLContainer; import org.testcontainers.junit.jupiter.Container; import org.testcontainers.junit.jupiter.Testcontainers; /** * Base class for integration tests using TestContainers. */ @SpringBootTest @Testcontainers public abstract class AbstractIntegrationTest { @Container static PostgreSQLContainer postgres = new PostgreSQLContainer<>("postgres:14-alpine") .withDatabaseName("testdb") .withUsername("test") .withPassword("test"); @DynamicPropertySource static void configureProperties(DynamicPropertyRegistry registry) { registry.add("spring.datasource.url", postgres::getJdbcUrl); registry.add("spring.datasource.username", postgres::getUsername); registry.add("spring.datasource.password", postgres::getPassword); registry.add("spring.flyway.enabled", () -> "true"); } } ``` **Step 3:** Update test to extend base class: ```java OrderRepositoryIntegrationTest.java src/test/java/com/ecommerce/order/repository/OrderRepositoryIntegrationTest.java package com.ecommerce.order.repository; import com.ecommerce.order.AbstractIntegrationTest; import com.ecommerce.order.constants.OrderStatus; import com.ecommerce.order.model.entity.Order; import com.ecommerce.order.model.entity.OrderItem; import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach; import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired; import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.jdbc.AutoConfigureTestDatabase; import org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.orm.jpa.DataJpaTest; import java.math.BigDecimal; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.util.List; import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*; /** * Integration tests for OrderRepository with real PostgreSQL. */ @DataJpaTest @AutoConfigureTestDatabase(replace = AutoConfigureTestDatabase.Replace.NONE) class OrderRepositoryIntegrationTest extends AbstractIntegrationTest { @Autowired private OrderRepository orderRepository; private Order order; @BeforeEach void setUp() { orderRepository.deleteAll(); order = new Order(); order.setCustomerId("CUST123"); order.setOrderDate(LocalDateTime.now()); order.setStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); order.setTotalAmount(new BigDecimal("59.98")); OrderItem item = new OrderItem("ITEM123", 2, new BigDecimal("29.99")); order.addItem(item); orderRepository.save(order); } @Test void testFindByCustomerId() { List orders = orderRepository.findByCustomerId("CUST123"); assertNotNull(orders); assertEquals(1, orders.size()); assertEquals("CUST123", orders.get(0).getCustomerId()); } // ... other tests } ``` --- ## Recommended Approach **Use Option 1 (application-test.yml)** because: ✅ **Centralized configuration** - all tests use the same settings ✅ **Fast execution** - H2 in-memory database is faster than containers ✅ **No Docker dependency** - tests run anywhere without Docker ✅ **Hibernate schema generation** - entities define the schema, ensuring consistency **Use Option 3 (TestContainers)** when: - Testing PostgreSQL-specific features (JSON columns, arrays, etc.) - Validating Flyway migrations in CI/CD - Integration tests that need production-like behavior --- ## Verification Steps After applying the fix: ```bash # Clean and rebuild mvn clean install # Run only repository tests mvn test -Dtest=OrderRepositoryTest # Run all tests mvn test # Verify with verbose output mvn test -X ``` --- ## Explanation ### Why Flyway Fails in Tests 1. **`@DataJpaTest` loads full JPA configuration** including Flyway auto-configuration 2. **Migration scripts use PostgreSQL syntax** (e.g., `SERIAL`, `TIMESTAMP`, specific data types) 3. **H2's PostgreSQL mode is not 100% compatible** - some syntax differences cause failures 4. **Tests don't need migrations** - Hibernate can generate schema from entities ### How the Fix Works **Option 1 (Recommended):** - Disables Flyway via `spring.flyway.enabled=false` - Uses Hibernate's `ddl-auto=create-drop` to generate schema from JPA entities - H2 database is created fresh for each test run - Schema matches entity definitions exactly **Option 2:** - Same as Option 1 but configured per test class - Useful when some tests need Flyway and others don't **Option 3:** - Spins up a real PostgreSQL container using TestContainers - Runs actual Flyway migrations against PostgreSQL - Slower but validates production behavior ### Trade-offs | Approach | Speed | Accuracy | Complexity | Docker Required | |----------|-------|----------|------------|-----------------| | **Option 1** | ✅ Fast | ⚠️ Good | ✅ Simple | ❌ No | | **Option 2** | ✅ Fast | ⚠️ Good | ⚠️ Medium | ❌ No | | **Option 3** | ⚠️ Slow | ✅ Exact | ⚠️ Complex | ✅ Yes | For unit/repository tests, **Option 1 is recommended**. For integration tests validating migrations, use **Option 3**. **`@DataJpaTest` doesn't auto-configure auditing** - Unlike full application context, slice tests need explicit configuration 3. **The database schema expects non-null values** for audit columns --- assertNotNull(orders); assertEquals(1, orders.size()); assertEquals("CUST123", orders.get(0).getCustomerId()); // ADDED: Verify audit fields are populated assertNotNull(orders.get(0).getCreatedAt()); assertNotNull(orders.get(0).getUpdatedAt()); } @Test void testFindByStatus() { List orders = orderRepository.findByStatus(OrderStatus.PENDING); assertNotNull(orders); assertEquals(1, orders.size()); assertEquals(OrderStatus.PENDING, orders.get(0).getStatus()); } @Test void testFindByCustomerIdAndStatus() { List orders = orderRepository.findByCustomerIdAndStatus( "CUST123", OrderStatus.PENDING);
